data.title: PRA Group prices €300 million of 6.250% senior notes due 2032
data.description:
• PRA Group (NASDAQ:PRAA) announced on Wednesday that its wholly-owned subsidiary PRA Group Europe Holding II S.à r.l. Luxembourg has priced its offering of €300 million aggregate principal amount of 6.250% Senior Notes due 2032. 
  • The offering of the notes is expected to close on or about September 30, 2025.
  • PRA Group intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay approximately $174 million of its outstanding borrowings under its North American revolving credit facility and approximately $174 million of its outstanding borrowings under its European revolving credit facility.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Franklin BSP Realty Trust prices $1.08B CLO, unlocks capital for $1B in new loan
data.description:
• Franklin BSP Realty Trust (NYSE:FBRT) priced a $1.076B commercial real estate CLO named BSPRT 2025-FL12.
  • The CLO has a 30-month reinvestment period, 88% advance rate, and a weighted average interest cost of SOFR+1.61%.
  • Settlement expected on October 15, 2025; FBRT will also call three older CLOs from 2021 to 2022 at that time.
  • FBRT will also finance a $500M asset pool via a money center bank.
  • The combined transactions are projected to: generate $250M in cash, reduce financing costs by ~65 bps, enable ~$1B in new loan originations.
  • Expected to contribute $0.05–$0.07 in incremental quarterly EPS.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: CleanCore Solutions registers nearly 199M Class B shares for potential resale by investors
data.description:
• CleanCore Solutions (NYSE:ZONE) has registered 198.82M shares of Class B common stock for potential resale by existing investors.
  • Includes: 168.3M shares already issued to selling stockholders, 30.5M shares issuable upon exercise of outstanding warrants.
  • Company will not receive proceeds from resale of shares but may gain funds if warrants are exercised.
  • ZONE shares down 3.4% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: PG&E appoints Alejandro Vallejo as Chief People Officer
data.description:
• PG&E Corporation (NYSE:PCG) said on Wednesday that it has appointed Alejandro Vallejo as executive vice president, chief people officer of the company as well as its subsidiary, Pacific Gas and Electric, effective Sept. 30. 
  • The company said Vallejo will lead PG&E's People organization, responsible for attracting and retaining the best workforce and fostering the right culture in building a safe, resilient energy system that meets customers' needs. This includes talent acquisition, labor relations, coworker development and change management.
  • Vallejo succeeds Kaled Awada, who announced his departure from PG&E earlier this month to pursue new opportunities, said the company. Before this role, Vallejo served as PG&E's Chief Risk Officer and Senior Vice President, Ethics & Compliance. 
  • PCG -0.06% after hours to $14.65.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Best Buy trims Geek Squad again to redirect resources
data.description:
• Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) is reducing its Geek Squad staff – the second round of layoffs in the department in less than 6 months --  in a bid to redirect company resources.
  • According to a Best Buy spokesperson quoted by Bloomberg, the number of employees impacted will be “very small.”
  • Best Buy's (NYSE:BBY) Geek Squad provides customers with technical support and repair assistance, a unique service provided by the electronics retailer.
  • However, in its most recent quarterly report, the company swung to a loss on weakness in its appliances and consumer electronics business, and kept full year sales and profit guidance unchanged despite peers hiking guidance to reflect a more favorable tariff environment.
  • Best Buy (NYSE:BBY) shares have lost 26% in value year-over-year versus a 16% gain in the S&P 500.

data.title: Boeing secures $198.02M U.S. Navy contract modification
data.description:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) was awarded a $198.02 million modification to a previously awarded cost-plus-fixed-fee, cost-plus-incentive-fee, indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract.
  • This modification increases the contract ceiling to provide service life modifications in support of extending F/A-18 Block II Super Hornet service life from 6,000 flight hours to 10,000 flight hours, as well as provides for integration of Block III avionics capabilities for the Navy.
  • Work will be performed in San Diego, California (44.4%); San Antonio, Texas (43.1%); and St. Louis, Missouri (12.5%), and is expected to be completed in February 2027.
  • Naval Air Systems Command, Patuxent River, Maryland, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Gold up more than 40% YTD, poised for biggest annual gain since 1979, Saxo says
data.description:
Gold prices are on course for their strongest one-year increase since 1979 after breaking above the $3,800/oz level, Saxo analysts said Wednesday, as futures have surged more than 40% so far this year, thanks to U.S. interest rate cuts, central bank buying and ETF inflows, as well as strong safe-haven demand due to persistent geopolitical uncertainties.

"The speed of the move underscores how shallow any corrections have been in recent weeks and months as dips continue to be met with fresh buying interest, a pattern consistent with broader macro support rather than speculative froth alone," Saxo said.

Meanwhile, silver's rally has been even sharper, topping $44/oz and surging more than 50% YTD, helped by structural demand growth from solar photovoltaics and electrification, as Saxo said silver's "dual identity as both a monetary and industrial metal has worked to its advantage.”

Gold futures pulled back Wednesday from record highs reached in the previous session, as investors awaited economic data due later this week for further clues on the Federal Reserve's policy path - weekly U.S. jobless claims data and the U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures index, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for September delivery settled -1.3% to $3,732.10/oz, its largest one-day dollar and percentage decline since August 11, and front-month Comex September silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.9% to $43.777/oz, still the third-highest close in history for both metals.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: KB Home delivers Q3 earnings beat, but trims guidance for housing revenue
data.description:
KB Home (NYSE:KBH) reduced its full-year guidance for housing revenue after its fiscal Q3 earnings and revenue beat the consensus estimate, even as they continued to decline from a year ago.

The company lowered its FY2025 guidance for housing revenue to $6.10B-$6.20B (Visible Alpha consensus $6.26B) from its prior view of $6.30B-$6.50B; average selling price to $483K ($484K Visible Alpha estimate) from $480K-$490K; and housing gross profit margin to 19.2%-19.3%, assuming no inventory-related charges vs. prior guidance of 19.0%-19.4%.

Fiscal Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.61, topping the average analyst estimate of $1.51, fell from $2.04 in last year's Q3.

Revenue for the quarter ended Aug. 31, 2025 was $1.62BB, beating the $1.59B consensus and declining from $1.75B a year ago.

KB Home (NYSE:KBH) stock edged down 0.2% in after-hours trading.

"“Our sales approach in the quarter remained consistent and straightforward, focused on offering homebuyers the most compelling value at a transparent price," said Chairman and CEO Mezger. "As the quarter progressed, we were encouraged to see affordability improving, as mortgage interest rates began to ease."

The Q3 average selling price of $475.7K vs. $488.7K in Q2 and $480.9K in last year's Q3.

Net orders fell 4.3% Y/Y to 2,950, while net order value dropped 15% to $1.31B. Its cancellation rate for the first nine months of its fiscal year rose to 17% of gross orders from 15% a year ago.

Homebuilding operating income margin was 8.1% vs. 10.8% a year ago. Housing gross profit margin fell to 18.2% from 20.6% a year ago, mainly due to reflecting price reductions, higher relative land costs, and geographic mix, partly offset by lower construction costs.

Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.

data.title: Range Impact raises $550,000
data.description:
• Range Impact (OTCPK:RNGE) has raised $550,000 comprising of a $350,000 investment by Tower IV, LLC, an investment vehicle of Joseph E. LoConti, the Company’s largest shareholder, a $100,000 investment by Edward Feighan, the company’s chairman of the board, and a $100,000 investment by Michael Cavanaugh, the company’s CEO.
  • The investors entered into securities purchase agreements pursuant to which they acquired a total of 3.67M shares of Range Impact’s common stock at a price of $0.15 per share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vor Biopharma registers 51M shares for resale linked to RemeGen deal and PIPE financing
data.description:
• Vor Biopharma (NASDAQ:VOR) has filed a prospectus to register 51M shares of common stock for potential resale by existing stockholders.
  • Includes: 16M shares issuable under a warrant granted to RemeGen as part of a June 2025 license agreement for telitacicept rights (outside Greater China) and 35M shares issuable from PIPE warrants issued in a June 27, 2025 private placement.
  • Vor will not receive proceeds from stockholder resales but will receive proceeds if warrants are exercised for cash.
  • The share numbers reflect a 1-for-20 reverse stock split completed on September 18, 2025.
  • Source: SEC filing
